chunks.text: The EECS Center for Student Affairs is led by Antoine Davis, Executive Director, who oversees undergraduate and graduate programs, student organizations, pre-graduate advising, tutoring referrals, and graduate student funding. The Director of Student Support oversees programs for EECS students and serves as the primary contact for graduate student matters, liaising with the College of Engineering Graduate Student Services Dean's Office. Judy Smithson, Director of Graduate Affairs, oversees EECS graduate policy and program administration. Michelle Fong, Director of Undergraduate Affairs, oversees EECS and CS undergraduate policy and program administration. Christopher Hunn, Director of Undergraduate Student Instruction, oversees undergraduate student instructors.
chunks.question: Who leads the EECS Center for Student Affairs?

chunks.text: Student Support includes Tiffany Grimsley, Assistant Director, who advises on student programs like the SUPERB Program and handles undergraduate and K-12 outreach. Charlene Duncan is the CS Scholars Program Director, advising prospective and current scholars. Graduate Admissions staff include Patrick Hernan for EE graduate admissions, fellowships, and GSI assignments, and Glenna Anton for CS graduate admissions and student support.
chunks.question: What is Tiffany Grimsley's role in Student Support?
chunks.answer: Assistant Director advising on student programs.

chunks.text: Graduate Advising staff include Judy Smithson as EE PhD Advisor, handling Electrical Engineering graduate student matters. Carissa Caloud is the CS PhD Advisor for Computer Science graduate students. Michael Sun and Tiffany Sun are Masters Degrees Advisors for 5th Year MS, MS-Only, and MEng programs, providing advising and monitoring student progress.
chunks.question: Who advises EE PhD students?

chunks.text: EECS Undergraduate Advisors include Gina Garcia as Program Lead and EECS Advisor, Kait Guthrie as ECE Lead Advisor and EECS Advisor, and Grayson Johnston as EECS Lead Undergraduate Advisor. They provide advising for EECS, ECE, and prospective students through email and appointment bookings.
chunks.question: Who is the EECS Lead Undergraduate Advisor?

chunks.text: CS Undergraduate Advisors include Lydia Raya, Assistant Director of Undergraduate Affairs; Charlene Duncan, CS Scholars Program Director; Carol Marshall, CS Student Advisor; Gina Garcia, CS Student Advisor; and Floriberto Garcia, CS Student Advisor. They offer advising and drop-in hours for CS students and scholars.
chunks.question: What is Lydia Raya's role for CS undergraduates?

chunks.text: Undergraduate Instruction Management is handled by Krystle Simon, EECS Course Manager. CS Class Scheduling and Enrollment includes Savannah Scarlett, CS Enrollment and Scheduling Manager, and Carlos Garcia, EECS Special Programs Manager. EE Class Scheduling and Enrollment is managed by Kait Guthrie, EE Scheduler and ECE Lead Advisor.
chunks.question: Who manages EE class scheduling?

chunks.text: Student Affairs Contacts provide email addresses for various inquiries: gradadmissions@eecs.berkeley.edu for prospective graduate students, mengadmissions@eecs.berkeley.edu for MEng program, prospective-ugradstudents@eecs.berkeley.edu for undergraduate major advising, grads-cs@berkeley.edu for current CS graduate students, grads-ee@berkeley.edu for current EE graduate students, eecs-advising@eecs.berkeley.edu for EECS/EE undergraduates, cs-advising@eecs.berkeley.edu for L&S CS undergraduates, and diversity@eecs.berkeley.edu for diversity and outreach questions.
chunks.question: Which email is for current EE graduate student advising?
